methods.description: This method involves statistical procedures to analyze the learning effects in bees, specifically focusing on their responses to conditioned stimuli (CS) and comparing acquisition curves using Generalized Estimating Equation (GEE) modeling.

steps.description: Subject responses were scored as binary variables.
steps.searchable_summary: Convert subject responses to binary variables

steps.description: Bees that responded to the CS odour on the first trial were excluded from the analysis.
steps.searchable_summary: Exclude bees with pre-existing appetitive memory from analysis

steps.description: GEE modeling was used to compare the acquisition curves of bees trained with different unconditioned stimuli.
steps.searchable_summary: Apply GEE modeling to compare acquisition curves

steps.description: Response to the CS was coded as the response variable with treatment and conditioning trial included as factors.
steps.searchable_summary: Code response to CS as response variable with factors

steps.description: Significance tests were based on Wald approximations of the likelihood ratio test.
steps.searchable_summary: Perform significance tests using Wald approximations

steps.description: Post-hoc least significant difference contrasts (LSD) were used to compare treatment groups.
steps.searchable_summary: Use LSD contrasts for post-hoc comparison of treatment groups

steps.description: Bees performance in the unrewarded tests was compared using Generalised Linear Models (GZLM).
steps.searchable_summary: Compare performance in unrewarded tests using GZLM
